title: dryopteris austriaca var. fructuosa
intro: Dryopteris austriaca var. fructuosa, also known as the Alpine Woodfern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, with the plant's roots and leaves said to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of the Alpine Woodfern include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate menstrual cramps, and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
